evals.prompt: Design a REST API for a pet adoption service. Resources: pets (dogs, cats), shelters, and adoption applications. A shelter has many pets, and an application links a user to a pet.
evals.expected_output: A valid OpenAPI 3.1 YAML spec with /shelters, /pets, /adoption-applications endpoints, snake_case properties, kebab-case paths, cursor pagination on list endpoints, OAuth2 security, and Problem JSON error responses.
assertions: info block includes x-api-id and x-audience
All list endpoints include cursor-based pagination parameters
Security scheme is OAuth2 with scopes defined

evals.prompt: I need an API spec for an internal invoice management system. We handle invoices with line items, each line item has a monetary amount. Invoices go through states: DRAFT, SENT, PAID, OVERDUE, CANCELLED.
evals.expected_output: A valid OpenAPI 3.1 spec using the common Money object for amounts, open-ended enum via examples keyword for invoice states in UPPER_SNAKE_CASE, proper date-time fields with _at suffixes, and company-internal audience.
assertions: Uses a Money schema with amount (decimal) and currency (ISO 4217)
Status field uses examples keyword for open-ended values (not closed enum)
Number types have explicit format (int32, int64, or decimal)

evals.prompt: Create an OpenAPI contract for a public-facing e-commerce product catalog. Needs: list products with filtering by category and price range, get product details, search products. Products have images (URLs), prices, and nested category hierarchy.
evals.expected_output: A valid OpenAPI 3.1 spec with GET /products (with query filter params), GET /products/{product_id}, POST /products/search for complex queries, proper pagination, and no URL versioning.
assertions: GET /products supports query parameters for filtering
A search endpoint uses POST with JSON body (not GET with complex query string)
Top-level responses are JSON objects (not bare arrays)
Pagination response includes cursor-based navigation links
